item.title: Java编程语言的优缺点 | Advantages And Disadvantages Of Java Programming Language
item.description:
有了这个优势，程序员只需编写一个 Java 程序并编译一次（从人类理解的语言转换为机器语言/字节码），然后结果可以在多个平台上运行而无需更改。 这一优势允许基于 java 的程序在 Linux 操作系统上运行，但在 Microsoft Windows 上运行良好。 迄今为止支持的平台是 Microsoft Windows 、 Linux 、 Mac OS 和

太阳索拉里斯。 原因是每个操作系统都使用自己的程序（可以从 Java 站点下载）来解释字节码。

  1. 完整的课堂图书馆

这个库的完整性与一个大型 Java 社区的存在相结合，该社区不断创建新的库来满足所有应用程序开发需求。

它具有类似于 C++ 编程语言的语法，因此吸引了许多 C++ 程序员转向 Java。 目前有很多Java用户，其中大部分是转向Java的C++程序员。 美国的大学也开始向新生教授 Java，因为它更容易让学生理解，并且对那些不主修计算机的人也很有用。

它具有自动设置内存使用的功能，因此程序员无需直接执行内存设置（如广泛使用的 C++ 语言中的情况）。

不足

一个平台和另一个平台之间仍然存在一些不兼容的东西。 对于 J2SE ，例如 SWT-AWT 桥接器，直到现在它还不能在 Mac OS X 上运行。

反编译是将当前代码转换为源代码的过程。 这是可能的，因为基于 Java 的代码是存储许多高级语言属性（例如类名、方法和数据类型）的字节码。 因此，程序使用的算法将更难隐藏，更容易被劫持/逆向工程。

  1. 内存消耗过多

基于 Java 的程序的内存使用量远大于上一代高级语言，例如 C/C++ 和 Pascal（更具体地说，是 Delphi 和 Object Pascal）。 通常这对于那些使用最新技术的人来说不是问题（因为安装内存的趋势越来越便宜），但对于那些仍然需要与超过 4 年的计算机进行斗争的人来说是一个问题。



原文:

Java Advantages

The main advantage of Java is that it can run on multiple platforms / computer operating systems , in accordance with the principle of write once, run anywhere . With this advantage the programmer simply writes a Java program and compiles (converted, from a human -understood language to a machine language / bytecode ) once and then the result can be run on multiple platforms without changes. This advantage allows a java -based program to work on the Linux operating system but run well on Microsoft Windows. Platforms supported to date are Microsoft Windows , Linux , Mac OS and

Sun Solaris . The reason is that each operating system uses its own program (which can be downloaded from a Java site) to interpret the bytecode .

  1. Complete Classroom Library

Java is known for its library equipment ( a group of programs included in java programming) that is very easy to use by programmers to develop their applications. The completeness of this library is coupled with the existence of a large Java community that is constantly creating new libraries to cover all application development needs.

It has syntax like the C ++ programming language so it attracts many C ++ programmers to move to Java. Currently there are many Java users, most of them are C ++ programmers who have moved to Java. Universities in the United States are also beginning to move by teaching Java to new students because it is easier for students to understand and can be useful as well for those who are not majoring in computers.

Automatically, it has the facility of setting memory usage so that programmers do not need to perform memory settings directly (as is the case in the widely used C ++ language).

Deficiency

There are still some things that are not compatible between one platform and another. For J2SE , for example SWT-AWT bridge which until now does not work on Mac OS X.

Decompilation is the process of turning from current code into source code. This is possible because Java-based code is a bytecode that stores many high-level language attributes, such as class names, methods, and data types. The same is true of the Microsoft .NET Platform . Thus, the algorithm used by the program will be more difficult to hide and easily hijacked/ reverse-engineered .

  1. Excessive Memory Consumption

Memory usage for Java -based programs is much greater than previous generation high -level languages ​​such as C/ C ++ and Pascal (more specifically, Delphi and Object Pascal ). Usually this is not a problem for those who use the latest technology (because the trend of installed memory is getting cheaper), but a problem for those who still have to struggle with computer machines older than 4 years.
item.author: deknurulaja

item.title: 如何使用无服务器框架节省 AWS Parameter Store | How to save up on AWS Parameter Store with serverless framework
item.description:
在我们的代码中使用引用 API 密钥或数据库密码的硬编码值是一种不好的做法，甚至存在安全风险。 或者，由于业务决策或不可预见的情况，我们的价值观可能会随着时间而改变； 这意味着每次需要更改值时我们都必须更改代码。 为此，我们可以使用名为 Parameter Store 的 AWS Systems Manager 服务。

我们可以将密码、数据库字符串、Amazon 系统映像 (AMI) ID 和许可证代码等数据存储为参数值。 我们可以将值存储为纯文本或加密数据。

但是，当我们必须使用多个变量时会发生什么？ 维护它们很困难，但是在几个开发环境中问题会加倍。 我们可以轻松达到每个账户和地区 10,000 个标准参数的硬性上限。

我们如何解决这个问题？

我们可以保存几个参数存储； 如果我们开始以 JSON 格式编写它们，我们可以让它们以清晰有序的方式组织起来。

让我们开始使用我们的 JSON 值创建一个 Parameter Store。 我将使用我的名字和姓氏作为此示例的值。

 /dev/myapp/INFO >> {"firstName":"David","lastName":"Llerena"}

使用无服务器框架创建一个 Typescript 无服务器 Lambda 项目。 让我们添加以下依赖项：

 //package.json
{ "name" : "myapp" , "version" : "1.0.0" , "description" : "" , "main" : "index.js" , "scripts" : { "test" : "echo \" Error: no test specified \" && exit 1" }, "dependencies" : { "aws-sdk" : "2.952.0" , "@types/aws-lambda" : "^8.10.95" , "serverless" : "^3.15.2" , "serverless-plugin-typescript" : "^2.1.2" , "typescript" : "^4.6.3" }, "author" : "" , "license" : "ISC"
}

现在让我们在名为helperScript.js的文件中创建一些 javascript 代码，创建一个将调用 SSM 服务并读取我们想要解析和读取 JSON 值的每个值的参数存储的函数。 在我们的serverles.yml文件中，我们将引用这个helperScript.js来调用环境语句下我们代码中使用的变量。 授予 lambda 访问 SSM 服务所需的权限。 建议仅授予对我们正在使用的资源的访问权限。

如果我们使用的是版本 3 次要的无服务器版本，我们必须在服务声明下添加此语句variablesResolutionMode: 20210326

 #serverless.yml service : myapp plugins : - serverless-plugin-typescript provider : name : aws runtime : nodejs16.x stage : dev region : us-east-1 environment : FIRST_NAME : ${file(./helperScript.js):getParameters.firstName} LAST_NAME : ${file(./helperScript.js):getParameters.lastName} iamRoleStatements : - Effect : " Allow" Action : - ssm:GetParameter - ssm:GetParameters - ssm:GetParametersByPath Resource : " *" custom : prefix : /${self:provider.stage}/${self:service} functions : hello : handler : handler.hello

最后，让我们在 lambda 代码中使用这个 Parameter Store。

 //handler.ts import { Handler } from ' aws-lambda ' ; export const hello : Handler = ( event : any ) => { const response = { statusCode : 200 , body : JSON . stringify ( { message : ' My First name is ' + ` ${ process . env . FIRST_NAME } ` + ' and my last name is ' + ` ${ process . env . LAST_NAME } ` }, null , 2 ), }; return new Promise (( resolve ) => { resolve ( response ) }) }

就是这样！，如果我们执行我们的 lambda，它应该是这样的：

这肯定会帮助我们节省一些参数存储并保持它们整洁。 但是，我们应该考虑这些变量可以获得的大小。 我们可能会考虑使用Advanced Parameters 。

参考： 无服务器框架变量



原文:

It is a bad practice and even a security risk to use hard-coded values within our code that reference an API Key or a Database Password. Alternatively, we can have values that can change over time because of a business decision or an unforeseen situation; this will mean that we have to change our code every time a value needs to be changed. For this, we can use the AWS Systems Manager service called Parameter Store.

Parameter Store is a capability of AWS Systems Manager that provides secure, hierarchical storage for configuration data management and secrets management. We can store data such as passwords, database strings, Amazon Machine Image (AMI) IDs, and license codes as parameter values. We can store values as plain text or encrypted data.

However, what happens when we have to use several variables? It is difficult to maintain them, but the problem doubles with several development environments. We can easily reach the hard cap limit of 10,000 standard parameters per account and region.

How do we solve this?

We can save several parameter stores; if we start writing them in JSON format, we can have them organized in a clear and organized way.

Let us start creating a Parameter Store with our JSON values. I will use my first and last name as values for this example.

/dev/myapp/INFO >> {"firstName":"David","lastName":"Llerena"}

Create a Typescript serverless Lambda Project with serverless framework. Let's add the following dependencies:

//package.json
{ "name": "myapp", "version": "1.0.0", "description": "", "main": "index.js", "scripts": { "test": "echo \"Error: no test specified\" && exit 1" }, "dependencies": { "aws-sdk": "2.952.0", "@types/aws-lambda": "^8.10.95", "serverless": "^3.15.2", "serverless-plugin-typescript": "^2.1.2", "typescript": "^4.6.3" }, "author": "", "license": "ISC"
}

Now let's create some javascript code in a file named helperScript.js, Create a function that will call SSM service and read a Parameter store which we would like to parse and read each value of the JSON values. ResolveConfigurationProperty statement will reference the stage and name of our service, declared in our serverless.yml file.

//helperScript.js
const SSM = require("aws-sdk/clients/ssm"); module.exports.getParameters = async ({ resolveConfigurationProperty }) => { const prefix = await resolveConfigurationProperty(["custom", "prefix"]); const region = await resolveConfigurationProperty(["provider", "region"]); const ssm = new SSM({ region }); const policy = await ssm .getParameter({ Name: prefix + "/INFO", WithDecryption: true, }) .promise(); const res = JSON.parse(policy.Parameter.Value); return { firstName: res.firstName, lastName: res.lastName };
};

In our serverles.yml file, we will reference this helperScript.js to call the variables used in our code under the environment statement. Give the lambda the necessary permissions to access the SSM service. It is recommended only to give access to the resources we are using.

If we are using a serverless version minor to version 3, we have to add this statement variablesResolutionMode: 20210326 under service declaration

#serverless.yml
service: myapp plugins: - serverless-plugin-typescript provider: name: aws runtime: nodejs16.x stage: dev region: us-east-1 environment: FIRST_NAME: ${file(./helperScript.js):getParameters.firstName} LAST_NAME: ${file(./helperScript.js):getParameters.lastName} iamRoleStatements: - Effect: "Allow" Action: - ssm:GetParameter - ssm:GetParameters - ssm:GetParametersByPath Resource: "*"
custom: prefix: /${self:provider.stage}/${self:service}
functions: hello: handler: handler.hello

Finally, let's use this Parameter Store in our lambda code.

//handler.ts
import { Handler } from 'aws-lambda'; export const hello: Handler = (event: any) => { const response = { statusCode: 200, body: JSON.stringify( { message: 'My First name is '+`${process.env.FIRST_NAME}`+' and my last name is '+`${process.env.LAST_NAME}` }, null, 2 ), }; return new Promise((resolve) => { resolve(response) })
}

And that's it!, if we execute our lambda it should look like this:

This will surely help us save up some Parameter Stores and keep them neat and clean. However, we should consider the size that these variables could get. We might take into consideration the use of Advanced Parameters.

Reference: Serverless Framework Variables
item.author: davidshaek

item.title: 为什么你没有生产力？ | Why you're not Productive?
item.description:
随着我的软件工程之旅的进展，我发现错误和缺点会减慢并降低我的生产力标准。 几年前好像没问题
直到我让自己完成一个自我评估过程！

今天我想分享一下那些常见的滑贴，让你感受一下，并在第一手准备好对抗它们

错误初始化（失去时刻）

作为一个人，我们会对发生在我们身上的一切做出反应。 无论是社交互动还是工作特定。

时间流逝的区域必须是理想的。 作为开发人员，如果给你分配了一个任务，那么应该有一个计划来实施并相处到终点线。

时间永远不会是无限的，所以如果你在正确的时间开始做事，成功的可能性比就会扩大。 你肯定有机会按时提交工作。 每天用 8 小时完成工作是一门艺术。 毫无疑问，您将通过时间和经验了解这一点。

如果您无法做到这一点，那么当您陷入深夜工作支出并确保您的屁股安全时，时间就快到了！
这直接对您的个人生活产生负面影响。 无论是您的家人、朋友还是您自己的空间。

缺乏优先级

主动和被动是开发人员内部的两种性质。 先手的人总是知道未来会发生什么，他/她计划并设置他们的调度程序，而其他人只是拖延。

这种做法导致没有目标成就或低进度水平。 对于那些正在努力寻找工作与生活之间更好契合点的人来说，这也是一个常见的问题。

没用的网上冲浪

开发人员喜欢与全球世界联系，这真的很棒。 但是明智地冲浪而不是盲目地冲浪。 如果您习惯在一个小时内多次滚动您最喜欢的应用程序的提要。 有可能破坏您的生产力栏。 明智地冲浪是指在特定时间冲浪以放松和缓解压力。 当您感到疲倦或沮丧时，观看播客、读书或看电影！

所以这就是现在的全部！

你遇到过这些问题吗？ 或者努力寻找更合适的。 让我知道内部评论，以便我们可以联系线程并一起学习！



原文:

As my software engineering journey progressed, I found mistakes and short comings that slowed and lowered down my productivity bar. Years before, it seemed no problem
until I put myself through a Self Assessment process!

Today I would like to share those common Slippery Patches so you can get a feel and prepare yourself against them on the first hand

False Initialization (Lose the moment)

Being a human, we respond to everything that comes in for us. Either its a social interaction or work specific.

Region of time lapse must be ideal. As a developer, if a task is assigned to you, there should be a plan to implement and get along to finish line.

Time can never be infinite, so if you initiate stuff at the right time the possibility ratio of success broaden itself. You definitely get a chance to submit work at time. Using 8 hrs to complete work daily is an Art. Undoubtedly, you'll learn this by time and experience.

If you are unable to do so, time is near when you get stuck in Late night spendings for work and get your ass safe!
This directly cast a negative impact on your personal life. Either your family, friends or your own space.

Lack of Prioritization

Active and Passive are two natures that live inside developers. The person with first always know what's ahead, he/she plans and set up their schedulers while others just procrastinate.

This practice leads towards No Goal Achievement or Low Progess Levels. Also a common problem of those who are struggling to find a better fit between Work and Life.

Useless Internet Surfing

Developers love to connect with global world and its really awesome. But surf wisely not blindly. If you're use to scroll feed of your favorite app, many times in an hour. Chances are there to spoil your productivity bar. By surfing wisely I mean to surf in a specific time to chill and relieve stress. Watch podcasts, read books or watch a movie when you feel tired or frustrated!

So thats all for now!!

Have you faced these issues? or struggling to find a better fit. Let me know inside comments so we can tie threads and learn together!

item.title: 各位开发者您好！ | Hello fellow developers!
item.description:
安东在线

发表于我很高兴能成为 dev.to 社区的一员！ 这里有一些很棒的人。 我喜欢每个人都可以分享想法并互相学习。

为什么不互相关注？ 学习新东西会很棒。

作为介绍：

我是 Linux、无服务器和代码的粉丝。 我白天在一家使用大量云技术的 SaaS 提供商工作。 晚上，我在一些小项目上随心所欲地编写代码，作为一种冥想形式。

Anto Online

item.title: Laravel API 系列：Laravel Sanctum 设置、注册、登录和注销 | Laravel API Series: Laravel Sanctum Setup, Sign Up, Login, and Logout
item.description:
在本系列的这一部分中，您将学习以下内容：

  • 圣所是什么
  • 如何安装和使用 Laravel Sanctum
  • 实现注册功能
  • 实现登录功能
  • 实现注销功能
  • 重组到受保护和公共的路线

在本节中，我将向您展示如何使用 Sanctum 对用户进行身份验证。

安装圣所

由于 Laravel 旨在提供出色的开发人员体验，因此您在本系列的第一部分生成的 Laravel 项目包括 Sanctum，您可以通过前往composer.json文件来确认，它应该在require数组中，如下所示：


绿色框是require数组。 如果您在composer.json文件的数组中找不到 Sanctum，请运行以下命令来安装它：

 composer require laravel/sanctum

上述命令将在您的应用程序中安装 Sanctum，您可以通过再次检查composer.json文件来确认。

创建个人访问令牌迁移

确认 Sanctum 的安装后，接下来是在数据库中创建个人访问令牌表，您可以通过在命令行中运行以下命令发布 Sanctum 的配置和迁移文件来实现：

 php artisan vendor:publish --provider = "Laravel \S anctum \S anctumServiceProvider"

上面的命令将在/database/migrations文件夹中创建一个create_personal_access_tokens_table.php并在/config文件夹中创建一个sanctum.php文件，一旦您验证了这两个文件的创建，接下来要做的就是迁移新的迁移文件，您可以使用以下命令执行此操作：

上面的命令会在你的数据库中添加一个新的personal_access_tokens表，检查你的数据库管理器来验证：

接下来，进入app/Http/Kernel.php文件，将middlewareGroups数组中的api数组替换为以下代码：

 'api' => [ \ Laravel\Sanctum\Http\Middleware\EnsureFrontendRequestsAreStateful :: class , 'throttle:api' , \ Illuminate\Routing\Middleware\SubstituteBindings :: class , ],

上面的代码是将用于验证我们的 API 的中间件。

接下来，我将向您展示如何在 Laravel 中保护路由。

保护路线

为了保护您的路由，您需要使用中间件函数对受保护的路由进行分组，如下所示：

 // posts routes Route :: group ([ 'middleware' => [ 'auth:sanctum' ]], function () { // protected routes go here });

上面的代码在Route Facade 上使用了静态group函数，并添加了中间件数组，该数组利用'auth:sanctum'中间件来保护您在函数内定义的路由。 为了向您展示它是如何工作的，我将在函数内添加所有发布路由，如下所示：

 // posts routes Route :: group ([ 'middleware' => [ 'auth:sanctum' ]], function () { Route :: resource ( 'posts' , PostController :: class ); Route :: get ( '/posts/search/{title}' , [ PostController :: class , 'search' ]); Route :: get ( '/post/author/{id}' , [ PostController :: class , 'get_author' ]); });

现在尝试通过向localhost:8000/api/posts发出 GET 请求来获取所有帖子，您应该得到以下结果：

绿色框是您从请求中获得的结果，它显示"message": "Unauthenticated." 路由已成功保护，现在您需要定义用户必须采取的步骤才能获得身份验证。 接下来，我们将定义signup函数。

注意：以上只是一个例子，后面我会重新构建所有的路由。

接下来，我将向您展示如何为与身份验证相关的功能设置控制器。

授权控制器

您在本系列的第二部分中学习了控制器用于组织应用程序中的功能，因此您需要创建一个包含与身份验证相关的所有功能的控制器。

首先，使用 artisan 创建一个控制器，将其命名为 AuthController，如下所示：

 php artisan make:controller AuthController

注意：您不应该添加--resource标志，因为我们不会在这里使用CRUD功能。

这应该创建一个包含以下代码的控制器文件：

 <?php namespace App\Http\Controllers ; use Illuminate\Http\Request ; class AuthController extends Controller { // }

接下来，添加所需的依赖项，在这种情况下将是：

 use App\Models\User ; use Illuminate\Http\Request ; use Illuminate\Http\Response ; use Illuminate\Support\Facades\Hash ;

在namespace App\Http\Controllers; 线。

  • 接下来，我将向您展示如何创建注册功能

    报名

    为了让用户能够登录，您需要创建函数。 因此，像这样创建一个公共sign_up函数：

     public function sign_up ( Request $request ){ }

    接下来，验证来自请求对象的数据，如下所示：

     $data = $request -> validate ([ 'name' => 'required|string' , 'email' => 'required|string|unique:users,email' , 'password' => 'required|string|confirmed' ]);

    上面的代码使用validate函数验证数据。

    • 名称是必需的字符串。
    • 电子邮件是必需的字符串，并且必须是用户表中列内的唯一值。
    • 密码是必填字符串，需要确认，所以用户需要再次输入。

    接下来，使用User模型上的静态create函数创建用户，如下所示：

     $user = User :: create ([ 'name' => $data [ 'name' ], 'email' => $data [ 'email' ], 'password' => bcrypt ( $data [ 'password' ]) ]);

    上面的代码使用带有先前data变量数组的create函数来创建用户。

    注意：上面数组中的password是用bcrypt函数包裹的，所以在将用户保存到数据库之前，密码会被散列。

    接下来，使用$user上的createToken函数生成身份验证令牌，如下所示：

     $token = $user -> createToken ( 'apiToken' ) -> plainTextToken ;

    上面的代码将创建一个令牌，该令牌将与每个请求一起发送到受保护的路由。

    接下来，创建成功创建用户后将发送回的响应：

     $res = [ 'user' => $user , 'token' => $token ]; return response ( $res , 201 );

    上面的代码创建了一个名为$res的变量，它是一个包含创建的user和生成的token的数组，并使用response函数返回它以及状态码201 ，这意味着创建了一个资源，即用户和令牌。 现在sign_up函数应该如下所示：

     public function sign_up ( Request $request ){ $data = $request -> validate ([ 'name' => 'required|string' , 'email' => 'required|string|unique:users,email' , 'password' => 'required|string|confirmed' ]); $user = User :: create ([ 'name' => $data [ 'name' ], 'email' => $data [ 'email' ], 'password' => bcrypt ( $data [ 'password' ]) ]); $token = $user -> createToken ( 'apiToken' ) -> plainTextToken ; $res = [ 'user' => $user , 'token' => $token ]; return response ( $res , 201 ); }

    接下来，为上述函数创建一个注册路由，如下所示：

     Route :: post ( '/signup' , [ AuthController :: class , 'sign_up' ]);

    注意：这条路线将是公开的。

    您现在可以通过将所需数据发送到/signup路由来创建用户，如下所示：

    • 紫色框是您将为此路由发送的请求类型，即POST请求。
    • 黄色框是路由localhost:8000/api/signup的 URL。
    • 红框是我发送给服务器的form-data格式的数据。
    • 绿色框是您成功发送请求后得到的结果 - 这将是创建的用户和生成的令牌。

    接下来，将生成的令牌添加为bearer token ，并向您之前定义的受保护路由发送 GET 请求：

    • 紫色框是您将为此路由发送的请求类型，即GET请求。
    • 黄色框是路由localhost:8000/api/posts的 URL。
    • 橙色框是我发送到服务器的令牌类型，即bearer token 。
    • 蓝色框是我发送到服务器的令牌，它是我注册时生成的令牌（这就是您在任何应用程序上注册后自动登录的原因）。
    • 绿色框是您成功发送请求后得到的结果——这将是数据库中的帖子，因为我没有通过身份验证，所以之前不可用。

    接下来，我将向您展示如何创建登录功能。

    登入

    您需要创建一个登录功能，以便用户可以登录。为此，请创建一个login功能，如下所示：

     public function login ( Request $request ) { }

    接下来，像这样验证请求数据：

     $data = $request -> validate ([ 'email' => 'required|string' , 'password' => 'required|string' ]);
    • 电子邮件是必需的字符串。
    • 密码是必需的字符串。

    接下来，检查用户是否像这样注册：

     $user = User :: where ( 'email' , $data [ 'email' ]) -> first (); if ( ! $user || ! Hash :: check ( $data [ 'password' ], $user -> password )) { return response ([ 'msg' => 'incorrect username or password' ], 401 ); }

    上面的代码执行以下操作：

    • 定义一个$user变量，其中包含具有给定电子邮件的用户。
    • 检查$user是否已注册，如果未注册，则返回'msg' => 'incorrect username or password'和401状态码。

    注意：401 状态码表示用户未经授权。

    接下来，如果电子邮件通过上述检查，则生成一个令牌，如下所示：

     $token = $user -> createToken ( 'apiToken' ) -> plainTextToken ;

    上面的代码会生成一个用于登录的令牌。

    接下来，创建将发送回用户的响应，如下所示：

     $res = [ 'user' => $user , 'token' => $token ]; return response ( $res , 201 );

    上面的代码创建了一个名为$res的变量，它是一个包含创建的user和生成的token的数组，并使用response函数返回它以及状态码201 ，这意味着创建了一个资源，在本例中为令牌。 现在login功能应该如下所示：

     public function login ( Request $request ) { $data = $request -> validate ([ 'email' => 'required|string' , 'password' => 'required|string' ]); $user = User :: where ( 'email' , $data [ 'email' ]) -> first (); if ( ! $user || ! Hash :: check ( $data [ 'password' ], $user -> password )) { return response ([ 'msg' => 'incorrect username or password' ], 401 ); } $token = $user -> createToken ( 'apiToken' ) -> plainTextToken ; $res = [ 'user' => $user , 'token' => $token ]; return response ( $res , 201 ); }

    接下来，为上述函数创建一个登录路由，如下所示：

     Route :: post ( '/login' , [ AuthController :: class , 'login' ]);

    注意：这条路线将是公开的。

    您现在可以通过将注册用户的电子邮件和密码发送到/login路由来登录，如下所示：

    • 紫色框是您将为此路由发送的请求类型，即POST请求。
    • 黄色框是路由的 URL： localhost:8000/api/login 。
    • 红框是我发送给服务器的form-data格式的数据。
    • 绿色框是您成功发送请求后得到的结果 - 这将是登录用户和生成的令牌。 接下来，将生成的令牌添加为不记名令牌，中提琴！ 您现在已通过身份验证，可以访问受保护的路由。

    接下来，我将向您展示如何创建注销功能

    登出

    在我们的例子中，登录函数是所有AuthController函数中最简单的。

    首先，创建一个公共logout功能，如下所示：

     public function logout ( Request $request ) { }

    接下来，您需要删除用户的有效令牌，您可以这样做：

     auth () -> user () -> tokens () -> delete (); return [ 'message' => 'user logged out' ];

    上述函数删除了登录用户的令牌，这意味着不记名令牌将不再起作用，用户将未经身份验证，并返回'message' => 'user logged out' 。

    现在，为注销功能创建一个路由，如下所示：

     Route :: post ( '/logout' , [ AuthController :: class , 'logout' ]);

    • 紫色框是您将为此路由发送的请求类型，即POST请求。
    • 黄色框是路由localhost:8000/api/logout的 URL。
    • 橙色框是我发送到服务器的令牌类型，即bearer token 。
    • 蓝色框是我发送到服务器的令牌，这是我登录时生成的令牌。
    • 绿色框是您成功发送请求后得到的结果——这将是从logout功能返回的消息。

    重组路线

    成功实现Signup、Login、Logout功能后，接下来就是将保护路由和公共路由分开。

    在这种情况下，您需要删除之前用于对CRUD路由进行分组的resource方法，因为create 、 update和delete路由现在将受到保护，因为未经身份验证的用户不应该能够创建、更新或删除帖子。

    所以api.php文件现在看起来像这样：

     // signup and login Route :: post ( '/signup' , [ AuthController :: class , 'sign_up' ]); Route :: post ( '/login' , [ AuthController :: class , 'login' ]); // public post routes Route :: get ( '/posts/search/{title}' , [ PostController :: class , 'search' ]); Route :: get ( '/post/author/{id}' , [ PostController :: class , 'get_author' ]); // public author routes Route :: get ( '/authors/search/{name}' , [ AuthorController :: class , 'search' ]); Route :: get ( '/author/posts/{id}' , [ AuthorController :: class , 'get_posts' ]); // private posts and authors routes Route :: group ([ 'middleware' => [ 'auth:sanctum' ]], function () { // private post routes Route :: post ( '/posts' , [ PostController :: class , 'store' ]); Route :: put ( '/posts/{id}' , [ PostController :: class , 'update' ]); Route :: delete ( '/posts/{id}' , [ PostController :: class , 'destroy' ]); // private author routes Route :: post ( '/authors' , [ AuthorController :: class , 'store' ]); Route :: put ( '/authors/{id}' , [ AuthorController :: class , 'update' ]); Route :: delete ( '/authors/{id}' , [ AuthorController :: class , 'destroy' ]); // logout Route :: post ( '/logout' , [ AuthController :: class , 'logout' ]); });

    就是这样！您已经成功实现了 API 的身份验证部分。 在下一部分中，我将向您展示如何在 Laravel 中测试 API。

    请使用评论部分的建议和反馈，我真的很感激。 我希望你喜欢这篇文章！

    这个系列的所有代码都可以在这里找到



原文:

In this part of the series, you'll learn the following:

  • What Sanctum is
  • How to install and use Laravel Sanctum
  • Implement the Sign-Up function
  • Implement the Login function
  • Implement the Logout function
  • Restructure the routes to protected and public

Laravel Sanctum setup

Laravel Sanctum, also commonly known as Sanctum is a lightweight authentication system used to authenticate token-based APIs and SPAs (ReactJs, VueJs, etc). In this section, I will show you how to authenticate users with Sanctum.

Install Sanctum

Due to Laravel's aim to provide a great developer experience, the Laravel project you generated in the first part of the series includes Sanctum, and you can confirm that by going to composer.json file, and it should be inside the require array like so:


The green box is the require array. If you can't find Sanctum inside the array in your composer.json file, run the following command to install it:

composer require laravel/sanctum

The above command will install Sanctum inside your app, and you can confirm by checking the composer.json file again.

Create personal access tokens migration

After confirming Sanctum's installation, the next thing is to create a personal access tokens table in the database, you do that by publishing Sanctum's configurations and migrations file by running the following in your command line:

php artisan vendor:publish --provider="Laravel\Sanctum\SanctumServiceProvider"

The above command will create a create_personal_access_tokens_table.php in your /database/migrations folder and a sanctum.php file inside the /config folder, once you have verified the creation of those two files, the next thing to do is to migrate the new migration file, and you do that with the following command:

The above command will add a new personal_access_tokens table to your database, check your database manager to verify:

Next, go to the app/Http/Kernel.php file and replace the api array inside the middlewareGroups array with the following code:

'api' => [ \Laravel\Sanctum\Http\Middleware\EnsureFrontendRequestsAreStateful::class,
'throttle:api',
\Illuminate\Routing\Middleware\SubstituteBindings::class, ],

The above code is the middleware that will be used to authenticate our API.

Next, I'll show you can how to protect routes in Laravel.

Protecting Routes

To protect your routes, you need to group the protected routes with a middleware function like so:

// posts routes
Route::group(['middleware' => ['auth:sanctum']], function () {
// protected routes go here
});

the above code uses the static group function on the Route Facade and adds the middleware array that utilizes the 'auth:sanctum' middleware to protect the routes that you define inside the function. To show you how this works, I'll add all the post routes inside the function like so:

// posts routes
Route::group(['middleware' => ['auth:sanctum']], function () {
Route::resource('posts', PostController::class);
Route::get('/posts/search/{title}', [PostController::class, 'search']);
Route::get('/post/author/{id}', [PostController::class, 'get_author']);
});

Now try to get all posts by making a GET request to localhost:8000/api/posts and you should get the following result:

The green box is the result you would get from the request, and it reads "message": "Unauthenticated.", and that's it! the route has been protected successfully, Now you need to define the steps the user has to take to get authenticated. Next, we will define the signup function.

Note: The above is just an example, I'm going to restructure all the routes later.

Next, I'll show you how to set up a controller for the functions related to authentication.

AuthController

You learned in the second part of the series that controllers are used to organizing functions in your application, So you'll need to create a controller that will contain all the functions related to authentication.

First, create a controller with artisan, name it AuthController like so:

php artisan make:controller AuthController

Note: You should not add the --resource flag, as we won't be using the CRUD functionality here.

That should create a controller file that contains the following code:

<?php namespace App\Http\Controllers; use Illuminate\Http\Request; class AuthController extends Controller
{ //
}

Next, add the dependencies required which in this case will be:

use App\Models\User;
use Illuminate\Http\Request;
use Illuminate\Http\Response;
use Illuminate\Support\Facades\Hash;

Add the code above under the namespace App\Http\Controllers; line.

  • The User is the user model and migration that was created when you generated your Laravel application.
  • The Request is the object that contains any data the user sends to the server.
  • The Response is the object that contains any data the server sends back to the user.
  • The Hash contains bcrypt function that will be used to hash the passwords.

Next, I'll show you how to create the Sign-Up function

Sign Up

For users to be able to sign in, you need to create the function. So create a public sign_up function like so:

 public function sign_up(Request $request){ }

Next, validate the data coming through the request object like so:

 $data = $request->validate([ 'name' => 'required|string', 'email' => 'required|string|unique:users,email', 'password' => 'required|string|confirmed' ]);

The above code validates the data using the validate function.

  • The name is a required string.
  • The email is a required string and has to be a unique value inside the column in the users table.
  • The password is a required string and needs to be confirmed, so the user needs to input it a second time.

Next, create user using the static create function on the User model like so:

 $user = User::create([ 'name' => $data['name'], 'email' => $data['email'], 'password' => bcrypt($data['password']) ]);

The above code uses the create function with an array of the previous data variable to create a user.

Note: The password in the above array is wrapped in bcrypt function, so the password will be hashed before saving the user to the database.

Next, generate an authentication token using the createToken function on the $user like so:

$token = $user->createToken('apiToken')->plainTextToken;

The above code will create a token that will be sent along with every request to a protected route.

Next, create the response that will be sent back once the user has been created successfully:

 $res = [ 'user' => $user, 'token' => $token ]; return response($res, 201);

The above code created a variable named $res which is an array that contains the created user and the generated token, and returns it using the response function along with the status code 201 which means that a resource was created, which is the user and the token. Now the sign_up function should look like so:

 public function sign_up(Request $request){ $data = $request->validate([ 'name' => 'required|string', 'email' => 'required|string|unique:users,email', 'password' => 'required|string|confirmed' ]); $user = User::create([ 'name' => $data['name'], 'email' => $data['email'], 'password' => bcrypt($data['password']) ]); $token = $user->createToken('apiToken')->plainTextToken; $res = [ 'user' => $user, 'token' => $token ]; return response($res, 201); }

Next, create a signup route for the above function like so:

Route::post('/signup', [AuthController::class, 'sign_up']);

Note: This route will be public.

You can now create a user by sending the required data to the /signup route, like so:

  • The purple box is the type of request you'll send for this route, which is a POST request.
  • The yellow box is the URL of the route localhost:8000/api/signup.
  • The red box is the data I sent to the server in form-data format.
  • The green box is the result you'll get after sending the request successfully - this will be the user that was created and the generated token.

Next, add the generated token as the bearer token and send a GET request to the protected routes you defined earlier:

  • The purple box is the type of request you'll send for this route, which is a GET request.
  • The yellow box is the URL of the route localhost:8000/api/posts.
  • The orange box is the type of token I sent to the server which is the bearer token.
  • The blue box is the token I sent to the server which is the token that was generated when I signed up(this is why you get logged in automatically once you sign up on any application).
  • The green box is the result you'll get after sending the request successfully - this will be the posts in the database which was unavailable earlier because I was not authenticated.

Next, I'll show you how to create the Sign-In function.

Sign In

You need to create a login function so users can log in. To do so, create a login function like so:

 public function login(Request $request) { }

Next, validate the request data like so:

 $data = $request->validate([ 'email' => 'required|string', 'password' => 'required|string' ]);
  • The email is a required string.
  • The password is a required string.

Next, check if the user is registered like so:

 $user = User::where('email', $data['email'])->first(); if (!$user || !Hash::check($data['password'], $user->password)) { return response([ 'msg' => 'incorrect username or password' ], 401); }

The above code does the following:

  • Define a $user variable that contains the user with the given email.
  • Check if the $user is registered and return 'msg' => 'incorrect username or password' with a 401 status code if it isn't.

Note: 401 status code means the user is unauthorized.

Next, generate a token if the email passes the above check, like so:

$token = $user->createToken('apiToken')->plainTextToken;

The above code generates a token that will be used to log in.

Next, create the response that will be sent back to the user like so:

 $res = [ 'user' => $user, 'token' => $token ]; return response($res, 201);

The above code created a variable named $res which is an array that contains the created user and the generated token, and returns it using the response function along with the status code 201 which means that a resource was created, in this case the token. Now the login function should look like so:

 public function login(Request $request) { $data = $request->validate([ 'email' => 'required|string', 'password' => 'required|string' ]); $user = User::where('email', $data['email'])->first(); if (!$user || !Hash::check($data['password'], $user->password)) { return response([ 'msg' => 'incorrect username or password' ], 401); } $token = $user->createToken('apiToken')->plainTextToken; $res = [ 'user' => $user, 'token' => $token ]; return response($res, 201); }

Next, create a login route for the above function like so:

Route::post('/login', [AuthController::class, 'login']);

Note: This route will be public.

You can now log in by sending the email and password of a registered user to the /login route, like so:

  • The purple box is the type of request you'll send for this route, which is a POST request.
  • The yellow box is the URL of the route: localhost:8000/api/login.
  • The red box is the data I sent to the server in form-data format.
  • The green box is the result you'll get after sending the request successfully - this will be the logged-in user and the generated token. Next, add the generated token as the bearer token, and viola! you are now authenticated and can visit protected routes.

Next, I'll show you how to create the Logout function

Logout

The login function is the simplest of all the AuthController functions in our case.

First, create a public logout function like so:

 public function logout(Request $request) { }

Next, you need to delete the user's valid token, and you do that like so:

 auth()->user()->tokens()->delete(); return [ 'message' => 'user logged out' ];

The above function deletes the token for a logged-in user, which means the bearer token will no longer work and the user will be unauthenticated, and returns 'message' => 'user logged out'.

Now, create a route for the logout function like so:

 Route::post('/logout', [AuthController::class, 'logout']);

  • The purple box is the type of request you'll send for this route, which is a POST request.
  • The yellow box is the URL of the route localhost:8000/api/logout.
  • The orange box is the type of token I sent to the server which is the bearer token.
  • The blue box is the token I sent to the server which is the token that was generated when I logged in.
  • The green box is the result you'll get after sending the request successfully - this will be the message that was returned from the logout function.

Restructuring the routes

After the Signup, Login, and Logout functions have been implemented successfully, the next thing is to separate the protected routes and public routes.

In this case it will be required that you remove the resource method that you used to group the CRUD routes earlier because the create, update and delete routes will now be protected because an unauthenticated user should not be able to create, update or delete posts.

So the api.php file will look like so now:

// signup and login
Route::post('/signup', [AuthController::class, 'sign_up']);
Route::post('/login', [AuthController::class, 'login']); // public post routes
Route::get('/posts/search/{title}', [PostController::class, 'search']);
Route::get('/post/author/{id}', [PostController::class, 'get_author']); // public author routes
Route::get('/authors/search/{name}', [AuthorController::class, 'search']);
Route::get('/author/posts/{id}', [AuthorController::class, 'get_posts']); // private posts and authors routes
Route::group(['middleware' => ['auth:sanctum']], function () {
// private post routes
Route::post('/posts', [PostController::class, 'store']);
Route::put('/posts/{id}', [PostController::class, 'update']);
Route::delete('/posts/{id}', [PostController::class, 'destroy']);
// private author routes
Route::post('/authors', [AuthorController::class, 'store']);
Route::put('/authors/{id}', [AuthorController::class, 'update']);
Route::delete('/authors/{id}', [AuthorController::class, 'destroy']); // logout 
Route::post('/logout', [AuthController::class, 'logout']);
});

And that's it!, you have successfully implemented the authentication part of the API. In the next part, I will show you how to test APIs in Laravel.

Please use the comment section for suggestions and feedback, I would really appreciate that. I hope you enjoyed the article!

All the code for this series can be found here
item.author: olodocoder

item.title: 使用 node 和 nginx Dockerize 一个 React 应用程序以及如何将图像推送到 Docker Hub | Dockerize a React App with node and nginx and how to push an image to Docker Hub
item.description:
让我们看看一个 React 应用程序 dockerized 以及如何将我们要创建的图像推送到 Docker Hub。

首先，如果您需要搜索主要的 docker 命令，请查看： https ://docs.docker.com/engine/reference/commandline

您需要创建一个具有写入、读取和删除权限的特定用户。 看看https://docs.docker.com/docker-hub/access-tokens/

在 docker hub 上创建访问令牌后：

 docker login -u <username>

创建一个 React 应用程序：

 npx create-app <app-name>

我们将使用https://hub.docker.com/_/node ，进入 src 文件夹，我们可以创建 Dockerfile 并复制这些命令。

 FROM node AS prod WORKDIR /app COPY package*.json ./ RUN npm install COPY . . # RUN npm test - if you want to test before to build RUN npm run build FROM nginx:alpine AS prod WORKDIR /usr/share/nginx/html COPY --from=prod /app/build . EXPOSE 80 # run nginx with global directives and daemon off ENTRYPOINT ["nginx", "-g", "daemon off;"]

进入 src 文件夹，您可以创建 .dockerignore 并添加它：

执行 Docker 镜像构建：

 docker image build -t react:v1 .

构建映像后，让我们运行以验证它是否正常工作。

 docker run -dit -p 3300:80 --name react react:v1

要获取容器 ID：

获得容器 ID 后，您可以使用它并提交容器：

 docker container commit <id-container> react:v1

让我们查找我们之前构建的图像并获取 id：

继续，我们需要提交：

 docker container commit 49cc7b3e3682 react:v1

查看docker images命令的输出，我们可以看到存储库名称、标签和图像 ID。 我们需要创建一个标签：

 docker tag <image-name:tag> <your-repository>/image-name:image-name-repository docker tag react:v1 luizcalaca/react:react

最后是推：

 docker push <repository>/<tag> docker push luizcalaca/react:react

联系人
电子邮件： luizcalaca@gmail.com
领英： https ://www.linkedin.com/in/luizcalaca/
推特： https ://twitter.com/luizcalaca



原文:

Let's see a React app dockerized and how to push the image the we are going to create to the Docker Hub.

First if you need to search the main docker commands, look at: https://docs.docker.com/engine/reference/commandline

You need to create one specific user with the write, read and delete privileges. Look at https://docs.docker.com/docker-hub/access-tokens/

After creating an access token on docker hub:

docker login -u <username>

Creating a React App with:

npx create-app <app-name>

We are going to use https://hub.docker.com/_/node, into the src folder we can create the Dockerfile and copy these commands.

FROM node AS prod
WORKDIR /app
COPY package*.json ./
RUN npm install
COPY . .
# RUN npm test - if you want to test before to build
RUN npm run build

FROM nginx:alpine AS prod
WORKDIR /usr/share/nginx/html
COPY --from=prod /app/build .
EXPOSE 80
# run nginx with global directives and daemon off
ENTRYPOINT ["nginx", "-g", "daemon off;"]

Into src folder, you can create the .dockerignore and add it:

Execute the Docker image build:

docker image build -t react:v1 .

After you built an image, let’s run to verify if it is working.

docker run -dit -p 3300:80 --name react react:v1

To get the container id:

After you got the container id you can use it and commit the container:

docker container commit <id-container> react:v1

Let’s looking for the image that we built before and get the id:

Going on, we need to commit:

docker container commit 49cc7b3e3682 react:v1

Looking at the output of the docker images command, we can see the repository name, tag and image id. We need to create a tag:

docker tag <image-name:tag> <your-repository>/image-name:image-name-repository

docker tag react:v1 luizcalaca/react:react

The last is the push:

docker push <repository>/<tag>

docker push luizcalaca/react:react

Contacts
Email: luizcalaca@gmail.com
Instagram: https://www.instagram.com/luizcalaca
Linkedin: https://www.linkedin.com/in/luizcalaca/
Twitter: https://twitter.com/luizcalaca

item.title: 你不是很一致，是吗？ | You're Not Very Consistent, Are You?
item.description:
蒂娜黄

发表于一致性意味着在保持长期愿景的同时专注于当前手头的任务。

一致性：

  • 允许测量
  • 建立问责制
  • 建立您的声誉
  • 让你相关
  • 维护您的信息

保持一致的好处

  • 反思我们的实践
  • 从事自我保健
  • 问候我们的学习者
  • 保持积极的课堂环境
  • 通过多元文化的视角进行教学
  • 提供有意义和有用的反馈
  • 帮助学生保留信息
  • 培养常规纪律
  • 建立信任的环境
  • 带着同理心和理解倾听
  • 为他人增值
  • 花时间培养与同事和学习者的关系
  • 拥抱安静的时间或正念
  • 激发好奇心并融入创造力的机会
  • 提供探索和探究的时间
  • 参与团队建设以促进沟通和协作
  • 利用社会情感学习
  • 培养一个学习环境，让每个人都了解他们的身份和他们的独特差异

一致性原则

一致性提示

  1. 提醒
  2. 当你完成最小的任务时奖励自己
  3. 立即纠正错误
  4. 使用激励工具继续前进
  5. 给自己时间看看复合变化
  6. 了解您的目标
  7. 知道你有什么时间来实现你的目标
  8. 找出最重要的下一步
  9. 在日程表中写下你的任务
  10. 每天采取行动

快乐编码！



原文:

Tina Huynh

Posted on May 15

Consistency means focusing on the task at hand in the present moment while maintaining a long term vision.

Consistency:

  • allows for measurement
  • creates accountability
  • establishes your reputation
  • makes you relevant
  • maintains your message

"The more you do something, the better you get at doing it. And when you get better, improving your performance gets easier."

“Small disciplines repeated with consistency every day lead great achievements gained over time” — John Maxwell

Benefits from Being Consistent

  • Reflecting on our practice
  • Engaging in self-care
  • Greeting our learners
  • Maintaining a positive classroom environment
  • Teaching through a culturally diverse lens
  • Providing meaningful and useful feedback
  • Helps students retain information
  • Develop the discipline of routine
  • Establish an environment of trust
  • Listening with empathy and understanding
  • Adding-value to others
  • Investing time in cultivating relationships with both colleagues and learners
  • Embracing quiet time or mindfulness
  • Inspiring curiosity and incorporating opportunities for creativity
  • Providing time for exploration and inquiry
  • Engaging in team-building to foster communication and collaboration
  • Leveraging social-emotional learning
  • Foster a learning environment that embraces every individual for who they are and their unique differences

Principles of Consistency

Tips for Consistency

  1. Reminders
  2. Reward yourself when you get even the smallest tasks done
  3. Correct mistakes right away
  4. Use motivational tools to keep going
  5. Give yourself time to see the compound changes
  6. Know the results you are aiming for
  7. Know what time you have available to word towards your goals
  8. Work out what's the most important next step
  9. Write your tasks in your schedule
  10. Take action each and every day

Happy coding!

item.title: 为了……的？ 还是为了……在？ 选哪个！ | For...of? Or for...in? Which to choose!
item.description:
迈克尔·鲍林

发表于需要遍历一些属性，但不确定选择哪个语句？ 我接到你了。

根据我们得心应手的 MDN，“ for...of语句创建了一个循环迭代可迭代对象”和“ for...in语句迭代对象的所有可枚举属性，这些属性由字符串键入。”

看看下面的例子。

对于...的：

 const superHeroes = ["Super Man", "Batman", "Hawkgirl", "Wonder Woman"] for(const superHero of superHeroes) { console.log(superHero) } // expected output: Super Man // expected output: Batman // expected output: Hawkgirl // expected output: Wonder Woman

对于...在：

 const heroIdentities = { "Clark Kent": "Super Man", "Bruce Wayne": "Batman", "Shayera Hol": "Hawkgirl", "Diana": "Wonder Woman" } for(const identity in heroIdentities) { console.log(`${identity} is: ${heroIdentities[identity]}`) } // expected output: Clark Kent is: Super Man // expected output: Bruce Wayne is: Batman // expected output: Shayera Hol is: Hawkgirl // expected output: Diana is: Wonder Woman

经验法则： for...of通常用于数组或类似数组的对象，而for...in通常用于对象。



原文:

Michael Bowlin

Posted on May 14

Need to iterate through some properties, but not sure which statement to choose? I got you.

According to our handy-dandy MDN, "the for...of statement creates a loop iterating over iterable objects" and "the for...in statement iterates over all enumerable properties of an object that are keyed by strings."

Take a look at the examples below.

for...of: 

const superHeroes = ["Super Man", "Batman", "Hawkgirl", "Wonder Woman"]

for(const superHero of superHeroes) {
  console.log(superHero)
}
// expected output: Super Man
// expected output: Batman
// expected output: Hawkgirl
// expected output: Wonder Woman

for...in: 

const heroIdentities = {
  "Clark Kent": "Super Man",
  "Bruce Wayne": "Batman",
  "Shayera Hol": "Hawkgirl",
  "Diana": "Wonder Woman"
}
for(const identity in heroIdentities) {
  console.log(`${identity} is: ${heroIdentities[identity]}`)
}
// expected output: Clark Kent is: Super Man
// expected output: Bruce Wayne is: Batman
// expected output: Shayera Hol is: Hawkgirl
// expected output: Diana is: Wonder Woman

Rule of thumb: for...of is typically used for arrays or array-like objects and for...in is typically used for objects.
item.author: jaidoutani

item.title: Next.js 图片组件备忘单 | Next.js Image Component Cheatsheet
item.description:
伊斯库尔巴诺夫

发表于在 Next.js 版本 10 中引入 Image 组件后，很少将 Next.js 与常规<img />组件一起使用，现在甚至被认为是不正确的！ 本文旨在帮助您学习和记住 Next.js Image 组件的最常见用例。

快速备忘单：

使用 layout 道具，您可以获得 5 个选项：

带布局填充（动态图像大小）

import Image from 'next/image' import example from '../asset/myimage.png' const Example = () => { return ( <Image src={example} alt="Alt text for the picture" layout="fill" objectFit="cover" quality={100} /> )

在下面的评论中，建议一些您自己喜欢/最常见的用例！

在BuildNextShop.com上了解有关 Next.js 的更多信息

马克·加西亚激流 - May 11 5月11日



原文:

iskurbanov

Posted on May 14

Cheatsheet for Next.js Image Component with common use cases

After the introduction of the Image component in Next.js version 10, it is rare to use Next.js with the regular <img /> component and is now even considered incorrect! This Article is to help you learn and remember the most common uses cases for the Next.js Image component.

Quick Cheatsheet:

1. with predefined width and height:

import Image from 'next/image'
import example from '../asset/myimage.png'

const Example = () => {
  return (
    <Image
      src={example}
      alt="Alt text for the picture"
      width="350px"
      height="300px"
    />
)

2. with predefined width and height with layout prop:

With the layout prop, you get 5 options:

'fill' 'responsive' 'intrinsic' 'fixed' and now 'raw'

import Image from 'next/image'
import example from '../asset/myimage.png'

const Example = () => {
  return (
    <Image
      src={example}
      alt="Alt text for the picture"
      width="350px"
      height="300px"
      layout="responsive" 
  />
)

with layout fill (dynamic image size)

import Image from 'next/image'
import example from '../asset/myimage.png'

const Example = () => {
  return (
    <Image
        src={example}
        alt="Alt text for the picture"
        layout="fill"
        objectFit="cover"
        quality={100}
   />
)

3. styling using Tailwind CSS

import Image from 'next/image'
import example from '../asset/myimage.png'

const Example = () => {
  return (
    <div className="relative w-24 h-24 border border-gray-200 rounded-md overflow-hidden">
        <Image
           src={product.image}
           alt={product.title}
           layout="fill"
           objectFit="cover"
       />
    </div>
)

4. Next.js Image as a background image

import Image from 'next/image'
import example from '../asset/myimage.png'

const Background = () => (
  <div>
    <ViewSource pathname="pages/background.js" />
    <div className="fixed h-screen w-screen overflow-hidden
  -z-10">
      <Image
        alt="Mountains"
        src="/mountains.jpg"
        layout="fill"
        objectFit="cover"
        quality={100}
      />
    </div>
  </div>
)

In the comments below, suggest some of your own favorite/most common use cases!

Learn more about Next.js at BuildNextShop.com

Marc Garcia Torrent - May 11
item.author: iskurbanov

item.title: Nghệ thuật dựng 组件 trong thực tế-Phần 4 | Nghệ thuật dựng component trong thực tế-Phần 4
item.description:
Bài viết được dịch từ: Bài viết được dịch từ:
https://ishadeed.com/article/building-real-life-components/ https://ishadeed.com/article/building-real-life-components/

Nội dung từ phải sang trái Nội dung từ phải sang trái

Trong trường hợp bố cục là LTR (Từ trái sang phải) và nội dung tin nhắn bằng tiếng Ả Rập, hướng của nội dung tin nhắn phải là RTL (Từ phải sang trái). Trong trường hợp bố cục là LTR (Từ trái sang phải) và nội dung tin nhắn bằng tiếng Ả Rập, hướng của nội dung tin nhắtrái).ải lài RTL (Từ trái sang phải)

Vì phần tử .card__content__start là một flex container, các item con sẽ tự động lật tùy thuộc vào direction của component hoặc phần tử gốc (root element). Vì phần tử .card__content__start là một flex 容器，các item con sẽ tự động lật tùy thuộc vào direction của 组件 hoặc phần tử gốc（根元素）。 Điều này có thể được thêm một cách chủ động tùy thuộc vào ngôn ngữ nội dung. Điều này có thể được thêm một cách chủ động tùy thuộc vào ngôn ngữ nội dung。 (Chú ý: css direction)

 <div class="card__content"> <div class="card__content__start" style="direction: rtl"></div> <div class="card__content__end"></div> </div>

Lật component Lật 分量

Nếu người dùng chọn RTL (Từ phải sang trái) như tiếng Ả Rập cho toàn bộ giao diện người dùng, thì component sẽ được lật. Nếu người dùng chọn RTL (Từ phải sang trái) như tiếng Ả Rập cho toàn bộ giao diện người dùng, thì 组件 sẽ được lật。

Vì flexbox được sử dụng để sắp xếp các mục, không cần thực hiện nhiều thao tác ngoại trừ việc lật margin. Vì flexbox được sử dụng để sắp xếp các mục, không cần thực hiện nhiều thao tác ngoại trừ việc lật margin。

 /* LTR */ .card__content__end { margin-left: 12px; } /* LTR */ .card__content__end { margin-right: 12px; }

Chi tiết về khả năng tiếp cận Chi tiết về khả năng tiếp cận

Sử dụng bàn phím Sử dụng bàn phím

Để xây dựng một sản phẩm hoạt động với hàng tỷ người dùng, nó phải hoàn toàn có thể truy cập được. Để xây dựng một sản phẩm hoạt động với hàng tỷ người dùng, nó phải hoàn toàn có thể truy cập được. Đối với component của bài viết này, tôi đã thử nghiệm trong Chrome và Firefox và nhận thấy các vấn đề sau: Đối với component của bài viết này, tôi đã thử nghiệm trong Chrome và Firefox và nhận thấy các vấn đề sau：

Nội dung từ phải sang trái

Trong trường hợp bố cục là LTR (Từ trái sang phải) và nội dung tin nhắn bằng tiếng Ả Rập, hướng của nội dung tin nhắn phải là RTL (Từ phải sang trái).

Vì phần tử .card__content__start là một flex container, các item con sẽ tự động lật tùy thuộc vào direction của component hoặc phần tử gốc (root element). Điều này có thể được thêm một cách chủ động tùy thuộc vào ngôn ngữ nội dung. (Chú ý: css direction)

<div class="card__content">
  <div class="card__content__start" style="direction: rtl"></div>
  <div class="card__content__end"></div>
</div>

Lật component

Nếu người dùng chọn RTL (Từ phải sang trái) như tiếng Ả Rập cho toàn bộ giao diện người dùng, thì component sẽ được lật.

Vì flexbox được sử dụng để sắp xếp các mục, không cần thực hiện nhiều thao tác ngoại trừ việc lật margin.

/* LTR */
.card__content__end {
  margin-left: 12px;
}

/* LTR */
.card__content__end {
  margin-right: 12px;
}

Chi tiết về khả năng tiếp cận

Rất tiếc, trong Chrome, tôi không thể truy cập menu tác vụ.

Danh sách cards

Trong danh sách card component, có một số role ARIA được sử dụng. Danh sách là ở dạng grid và nó chứa các hàng. Mỗi hàng có thể có một hoặc nhiều ô.

item.author: buiminh15

item.title: 管理团队的压力 | Managing a team's stress
item.description:
作为领导者，可能很容易推动您的团队充分利用他们并取得成果。 人们不喜欢说不，通常只想提供帮助并全力以赴。 许多人甚至会提议在个人日开会或稍后工作一两个星期。 虽然尊重人们的隐私和他们管理自己生活的权利很重要，但你也有责任尽你所能阻止倦怠。

我发现解决这个问题的最佳方法是限制工作中不必要的压力，让你的耳朵非常靠近地面，并使用烟雾信号。 一些策略

  • 举个例子，我曾经看到一位经理懒洋洋地留下一张纸条，说他们将在那天休假，因为他们“醒来时脾气暴躁”。 这是了不起的领导力，它鼓励其他人也这样做，并使心理健康日的想法正常化。

  • 尝试强制休假； 我的上一个角色有灵活的 PTO，所以它最初是为了防止人们不接受它。 除了最低限度之外，我们说它的一部分必须是连续的。 我明确表示，如果人们拿不到足够的钱，我会从奖金中扣除。

  • 清晰透明。 不要让人们对未知事物感到压力。 虽然不断地重新确定优先级是一种压力源，但一旦你知道了一些事情，加入团队并没有什么坏处。如果你设定期望，尽可能地定义它们，并让自己在最终仍然不清楚时回答问题.

  • 下班后不要写或回复电子邮件； 我可能正在工作，但我不想宣传（当然也不会美化）它或强迫人们做出回应。 尊重人们的隐私。 让人们知道他们不需要给出请假的理由，并确保不要询问细节，除非人们自愿提供。

  • 如果有人在下班时间工作，请到场。 在我的工作中，很少有人不得不在晚上或周末工作。 在我们需要的几次中，我确保也（亲自或虚拟地）在那里。

  • 烟火信号; 除了传统的 1-1s 和 Retros，我每周进行一次会议，我会拍摄一堆 GIF 并将它们放在 miro board 上，然后每个人都会四处讨论他们最喜欢哪一个。 我有一个“愚蠢的经理”的想法，并创建了一个仪表板来模仿巴西牛排馆，（如果你需要做更多的事情，就放一张绿卡，如果你吃饱了就放一张红卡），没有人真正使用它，但我没有不后悔尝试。

在一天结束的时候，人们将过自己的生活。 压力可能来自很多地方，虽然你永远无法完全消除它，但如果可以的话，尽量减少它并没有什么坏处。 一个压力较小的团队会更有效、更快乐，离开的可能性更小，只会带来更有趣的环境。



原文:

As a leader, it can be tempting to push your team to get the most out of them and to get results. People don't like like saying no and often just want to be helpful and give it their all. Many people will even offer to do things like take a meeting on a personal day or work a bit later for a week or two. While it's important to respect people's privacy and their right to manage their own lives, it's also your responsibility to do what you can to discourage burnout.

I’ve found the best ways to manage this is to limit the amount of unnecessary stress at work, keep your ear very close to the ground, and use smoke signals. Some strategies

  • Lead by example, I once saw a manager leave a note in slack saying they were going to take that day off because they "woke up grumpy". That's awesome leadership, it encourages others to do the same and normalizes the idea of a mental health day.

  • Experiment with mandatory time off; my last role had flexible PTO so it was originally a safeguard to prevent people from not taking it. Apart from a minimum, we said that part of it must be consecutive. I made it clear that I would deduct from bonuses if people don't take enough.

  • Be clear and transparent. Don't make people stress out about unknowns. While constant reprioritization is a stressor, once you know about something it doesn't hurt to fill the team in. If you are setting expectations define them as much as possible and make yourself around to answer questions if/when there ends up still being unclarity.

  • Don't write or respond to emails after work hours; I may be working but I don't want to publicize (and certainly not glorify) it or compel people to respond. Gmail has a feature to schedule emails for the next day, so does slack.

  • Respect people’s privacy. Let people know they don't need to give a reason for taking time off and make sure not to ask for details unless people volunteer them.

  • Be there if someone is working off-hours. In my line of work, it was very rare for anyone to have to work nights or weekends. For the few times we needed to I made sure to be there (in person or virtually) as well.

  • Smoke signals; besides traditional 1-1s and retros, I did a weekly session where I take a bunch of gifs and put them on a miro board, then everyone goes around and talks about which one they feel most like. I had a "dumb manager" idea and created a dashboard to emulate a Brazilian steakhouse, (put up a green card if you need more stuff to do, put on a red if you are full), no one really used it but I don't regret trying.

At the end of the day, people will live their own lives. Stress can come from many places and while you can never eliminate it completely, it doesn't hurt to try to reduce it if you can. A less stressful team is more effective, happier, less likely to leave and just results in a more fun environment.
item.author: jberkowsky

item.title: JavaScript - 地图 | JavaScript - Map
item.description:
嘿👋，希望你们一切都好！ 让我们来谈谈 JavaScript 中的Map ！

地图🗺️

但是等等……这和 JS 中的Object不一样吗？ 当您使用typeof时，Map 确实返回Object ，但并不完全相同。 它们在性质上非常相似，但它们具有决定其用途的明显差异。

那么这些明显的区别是什么？ 好吧，如果您使用Object ，则键必须是String或Symbol数据类型，而Map中的键可以是任何数据类型，包括Function和Object 。 另一件事是，要找到Object的大小，您必须手动完成，例如使用Object.keys()获取所有键，然后计算长度。 但是， Map有一个属性.size ，它返回Map的大小。

此外，对象不能直接迭代，而使用 Maps 可以。

示例： Map带有自己的方法，您可以在下面看到其中的一些。

 const cypher = { name : " Cypher " , color : " white " }; const raze = { name : " Raze " , color : " orange " }; const omen = { name : " Omen " , color : " blue " }; const characterRoles = new Map (); // create an empty Map. // add new data characterRoles . set ( cypher , " sentinel " ); // you can chain the method to add multiple entries characterRoles . set ( raze , " duelist " ) . set ( omen , " smokes " ); // check size characterRoles . size // 3 // iterable characterRoles . forEach (( role , char ) => { console . log ( char . name + " is a " + role ) }); // Cypher is a sentinel // Raze is a duelist // Omen is a smokes

随着我继续学习更多关于数据结构和算法的知识，我了解到Map是 JavaScript 的等价物，并且一直在使用它们来解决一些问题。

概括

总而言之，Map 允许您将数据存储为键值对，而不仅限于String （或Symbol ）数据类型作为键。 它还使您能够直接遍历数据。 此外，地图有它自己的属性和方法，您可以使用它们来添加、访问、编辑和删除集合中的数据。

感谢您阅读这篇短文，如果您想添加信息或提供反馈，请发表评论😀。



原文:

Hey 👋, hope y'all are well! Lets talk about Map in JavaScript!

Map 🗺️

Map in JavaScript (JS) allow you to store information/data using a key-value relationship. But wait... isn't that the same as an Object in JS? Map does return Object when you use typeof but it is not exactly the same. They are very similar in nature but they have distinct differences that determine their uses.

So what are these distinct differences? Well, if you are using Object the keys must be a String or Symbol datatype whereas the keys in Map can be any datatype including Function and Object. Another thing is that to find the size of an Object you must do it manually, e.g. grabbing all the keys using Object.keys() then counting the length. But, Map has a property .size which return the size of the Map.

Additionally, Objects are not directly iterable while with Maps you can.

Example: Map comes with its own methods and you can see some of them below.

const cypher = {name: "Cypher", color: "white"};
const raze = {name: "Raze", color: "orange"};
const omen = {name: "Omen", color: "blue"}; const characterRoles = new Map(); // create an empty Map. // add new data characterRoles.set(cypher, "sentinel"); // you can chain the method to add multiple entries
characterRoles.set(raze, "duelist") .set(omen, "smokes"); // check size
characterRoles.size // 3 // iterable
characterRoles.forEach((role, char) => { console.log(char.name + " is a " + role)
}); // Cypher is a sentinel
// Raze is a duelist
// Omen is a smokes

As I continue to learn more about Data Structure and Algorithm, I learnt that Map is JavaScript's equivalent to a Hashmap and have been using them to solve some problems.

Summary

To summarise, Map allow you to store data as key-value pairs that are not limited to String (or Symbol) datatype as keys. It also give you the ability to directly iterate through the data. Additionally, Maps have it's own properties and methods that you can use to add, access, edit, and delete the data in the collection.

Thank you for reading this short post, please leave a comment if you want to add information or give feedback 😀.
item.author: justtanwa

item.title: 反应 useEffect 依赖警告？！ | React useEffect Dependency Warnings?!
item.description:
我即将结束 Flatiron 软件工程训练营的第 2 阶段。 我真的很喜欢学习道具、状态、事件、受控组件、副作用、客户端路由等。然而，React 的一个领域给我带来了一些困惑...... ESLint 不断警告我缺少依赖项时使用 useEffect 挂钩。

使用效果

为了防止每次渲染都调用该函数（例如，当我们只想从 API 获取数据一次时），我们为 useEffect 提供了一个依赖数组。

在上面的示例中，我为 useEffect 挂钩提供了一个空数组作为其依赖项。 这告诉 React 它只需要运行这个函数一次。

但是，如果我们在 useEffect 钩子中的函数包含道具和/或状态，我们需要将它们作为依赖项添加到依赖项数组中。

在此示例中，请注意我仅包含“currentAircraft”状态作为唯一依赖项。 这就是 React 要求我们包含的所有内容。 但是，ESLint 会在依赖数组下划线，并显示消息React Hook useEffect has a missing dependency: 'setCurrentAircraft'. Either include it or remove the dependency 将 setter 函数作为依赖项来安抚警告并没有什么坏处。 但我想找到一个更好的解决方案。

经过一些论坛和文档搜索，我在这个堆栈溢出帖子中找到了一个很好的解决方案。 在得分最高的解决方案中，Shubham Khatri 建议在依赖项行上方添加eslint-disable-next-line注释。 所以我们上面的例子现在看起来像这样：

通过 ESLint github 阅读，似乎 ESLint 无法确定依赖项是否是 setter 函数。 至少现在还没有。 因此，如果您不喜欢错误警告的唠叨，则此解决方法是必要的。 我想你可以忽略警告:)

如果你有不同的方法来处理这个警告，或者我对警告的解释有点不对，请在评论中告诉我！ 毕竟，我还不是 React 专家。

劳塔罗·安德烈尼 ( Lautaro Andreani)在Unsplash上拍摄的照片



原文:

I'm nearing the end of phase 2 of the Flatiron Software Engineering bootcamp. React was the focus of this phase and, honestly, it was a lot of fun. I really enjoyed learning about props, states, events, controlled components, side effects, client-side routing, etc. However, there was one area of React that was causing some confusion for me... ESLint kept warning me of missing dependencies when using the useEffect hook.

useEffect

The useEffect hook allows us to call a function that is passed into it whenever a component renders. To prevent the function from being called with every render (e.g. when we want to fetch data from an API only once), we provide useEffect with a dependencies array.

In the example above, I'm providing the useEffect hook with an empty array as its dependency. This tells React that it only needs to run this function once.

ESLint Dependency Warning

If, however, our function inside the useEffect hook contains props and/or states, we need to add these as dependencies within the dependency array.

In this example, notice that I'm only including the "currentAircraft" state as the sole dependency. This is all React requires us to include. The setter function "setCurrentAircraft" isn't a state or a prop, it's a function and therefore, is not a dependency. However, ESLint will underline the depenedency array with the message React Hook useEffect has a missing dependency: 'setCurrentAircraft'. Either include it or remove the dependency. It doesn't hurt to include the setter function as a dependency to appease the warning. But I wanted to find a better solution.

After some forum and documentation searching, I found a great solution in this stack overflow post. In the highest scoring solution, Shubham Khatri recommends adding the eslint-disable-next-line comment above the dependencies line. So our example above would now look like this:

Reading through the ESLint github, it seems that ESLint isn't able to determine if a dependency is a setter function. At least not yet. Therefore, this workaround is necessary if you dislike the nagging of false warnings. I suppose you could just ignore the warning :)

If you have a different way to deal with this warning, or my interpretation of the warning is a little off, please let me know in the comments! After all, I'm no React expert... yet.

Photo by Lautaro Andreani on Unsplash
item.author: nicm

item.title: 强制发送 ETH - 2 | Force Send ETH - 2
item.description:
鲁尚克学者

发表于在上一篇文章Force Send ETH - 1中，我们了解了selfdestruct以及它如何用于将 ETH 强制发送到任何合约。

现在让我们举个例子来了解其中的漏洞是什么。 考虑以下合同：

 contract Crowdfund { // this contract only accepts a certain amount of funds // if someone tries to send an amount that exceeds contract balance more than the limit, tnx fails // funds are transfered to an address only when contract balance equals fund limit uint fundLimit = 3 ether ; bool contractOpen = true ; function donate () external payable { // for others to donate eth to this contract require ( contractOpen , "Contract has stopped recieving funds" ); require ( address ( this ). balance <= fundLimit , "Can't send specified amount" ); // note: we cannot do address(this).balance + msg.value, because address(this).balance already takes msg.value } function getBalance () external view returns ( uint ) { // to get current balance of this contract return address ( this ). balance ; } function sendFunds () external { // to send all collected funds require ( contractOpen , "Contract has stopped recieving funds" ); require ( address ( this ). balance == fundLimit , "Fund limit not reached yet" ); contractOpen = false ; // contract closed payable ( address ( 0x78731D3Ca6b7E34aC0F824c42a7cC18A495cabaB )). transfer ( address ( this ). balance ); } }

现在考虑以下场景：

  • 合约余额已达到 3 个以太币，但一些黑客强行向 CrowdFund 合约地址发送了额外的 ETH。
  • 现在合约余额>fundLimit，因此require(address(this).balance == fundLimit, "Fund limit not reached yet"); 希望您对漏洞的确切含义有所了解。

    让我们看看攻击者合约：

     contract Attacker { fallback () external payable { } function attack ( address _crowdFund ) external { selfdestruct ( payable ( _crowdFund )); } }

    到目前为止，我们了解了这个漏洞到底是什么以及黑客如何利用它。 现在让我们看看如何避免此类黑客攻击。

    • 一种解决方案是改变

    require(address(this).balance == fundLimit, "Fund limit not reached yet"); 到

    require(address(this).balance >= fundLimit, "Fund limit not reached yet"); 在众筹合约的Crowdfund sendFunds()函数中。

    但是，如果这是某些应用程序的重要条件呢？

    • 更好的解决方案是避免使用address(this)来跟踪资金

    让我们看看下面的合同来更好地理解这一点：

     contract CrowdFund_safe { uint fundLimit = 3 ether ; bool contractOpen = true ; uint balance = 0 ; function donate () external payable { // for others to donate eth to this contract require ( contractOpen , "Contract has stopped recieving funds" ); require ( balance + msg . value <= fundLimit , "Can't send specified amount" ); balance += msg . value ; } function getBalance () external view returns ( uint ) { // to get current balance of this contract return address ( this ). balance ; } function sendFunds () external { // to send all collected funds require ( contractOpen , "Contract has stopped recieving funds" ); require ( balance == fundLimit , "Fund limit not reached yet" ); contractOpen = false ; // contract closed payable ( address ( 0x78731D3Ca6b7E34aC0F824c42a7cC18A495cabaB )). transfer ( balance ); } }

    现在即使黑客强行向该合约发送 ETH，它也不会改变balance状态变量，因此这不会影响合约中的任何条件。
    因此，合约可以免受此类攻击。



原文:

Rushank Savant

Posted on May 14

In the last post Force Send ETH - 1, we understood about selfdestruct and how it's used to forcefully send ETH to any contract.

Now let's take an example to understand what's the vulnerability in this. Consider the following contract:

contract Crowdfund { // this contract only accepts a certain amount of funds
 // if someone tries to send an amount that exceeds contract balance more than the limit, tnx fails
 // funds are transfered to an address only when contract balance equals fund limit
 uint fundLimit = 3 ether; bool contractOpen = true; function donate() external payable { // for others to donate eth to this contract
 require(contractOpen, "Contract has stopped recieving funds"); require(address(this).balance <= fundLimit, "Can't send specified amount"); // note: we cannot do address(this).balance + msg.value, because address(this).balance already takes msg.value
 } function getBalance() external view returns(uint) { // to get current balance of this contract
 return address(this).balance; } function sendFunds() external { // to send all collected funds
 require(contractOpen, "Contract has stopped recieving funds"); require(address(this).balance == fundLimit, "Fund limit not reached yet"); contractOpen = false; // contract closed
 payable(address(0x78731D3Ca6b7E34aC0F824c42a7cC18A495cabaB)).transfer(address(this).balance); }
}

Now consider the following scenario:

  • Contract balance has reached 3 ether, but some hacker force-sends extra ETH to CrowdFund contract address.
  • Now contract balance > fundLimit, hence require(address(this).balance == fundLimit, "Fund limit not reached yet"); from the sendFunds() function will fail and not let anyone send the collected funds to required receiver.

Hope you got an idea about what exactly the vulnerability is.

Let's see the attacker contract:

contract Attacker{ fallback() external payable { } function attack(address _crowdFund) external { selfdestruct(payable(_crowdFund)); }
}

Till now we understood what exactly this vulnerability is and how hackers can take advantage of it. Let's now see how to avoid such hacks.

  • one solution is to change

require(address(this).balance == fundLimit, "Fund limit not reached yet"); to

require(address(this).balance >= fundLimit, "Fund limit not reached yet"); in sendFunds() function of Crowdfund contract.

But what if that's an important condition for some application?

  • better solution is to avoid using address(this) to track funds

Let's see the following contract to understand this better:

contract CrowdFund_safe{ uint fundLimit = 3 ether; bool contractOpen = true; uint balance = 0; function donate() external payable { // for others to donate eth to this contract
 require(contractOpen, "Contract has stopped recieving funds"); require(balance + msg.value <= fundLimit, "Can't send specified amount"); balance += msg.value; } function getBalance() external view returns(uint) { // to get current balance of this contract
 return address(this).balance; } function sendFunds() external { // to send all collected funds
 require(contractOpen, "Contract has stopped recieving funds"); require(balance == fundLimit, "Fund limit not reached yet"); contractOpen = false; // contract closed
 payable(address(0x78731D3Ca6b7E34aC0F824c42a7cC18A495cabaB)).transfer(balance); }
}

Now even if hacker force-sends ETH to this contract, it won't change the balance state variable, hence this won't affect any conditions in the contract.
Hence the contract is safe from such attacks.
item.author: rushanksavant

item.title: 因此，您可以在 AssemblyScript 中进行基准测试... | So, you can benchmark in AssemblyScript...
item.description:
介绍... Astral -- 用于 AssemblyScript 的简约基准测试库。 基于criteria.rs。
使用这个新的基准测试工具，我们能够轻松地在我们的代码中精确地可视化和优化性能。 通常，我们必须使用 while 循环和Date.now或performance.now绑定来衡量性能。 让我们开始吧！ 在现有的 AssemblyScript 项目中，安装 as-tral。

接下来，在您的程序集目录中创建一个benches文件夹。 在其中添加一个名为 as-tral.d.ts 的文件。

 assembly/ └── __benches__/ └── as-tral.d.ts

在 as-tral.d.ts 中，复制粘贴

/// <reference path="../../node_modules/@as-tral/cli/as-tral.d.ts" />

将任何带有 .ts 扩展名的文件粘贴在benches中。 你甚至可以拥有多个。

 assembly/ └── __benches__/ ├── as-tral.d.ts └── my-benchmark.ts

您的基准将存在于此文件中。 让我们创建一个示例基准。

 // to ensure accurate benchmarks, we must make sure that binaryen doesn't do any sneaky // optimizations on our input without us knowing. Thus, we must use `blackbox`. const input = blackbox ( " The quick brown fox jumped over the lazy dog. " . repeat ( 10 )); // our string here must be a compile time constant. // open an issue if you'd like to see this constraint lifted. bench ( " string split " , () => { // this function body will be run many times. // we must make sure our compiler won't throw away the computation, // so we use `blackbox` here again. blackbox ( input . split ( " " )); });

现在，让我们运行 as-tral。

 rom@i9-cabin:~/demo$ npx astral Compiling assembly/__benches__/hello.ts Benchmarking string split: Warming up for 3000ms Benchmarking string split: Collecting 100 samples in estimated 5018.6ms (1.2M iterations) Benchmarking string split: Analyzing string split time: [3770.9ns 3775ns 3778.9ns] Found 4 outliers among 100 measurements (4%) 3 (3%) low mild 1 (1%) high mild

相当棒！

博主和程序员 - May 9 5月9日



原文:

Introducing... Astral--A minimalistic benchmarking library for AssemblyScript. Based off of criterion.rs.
With this new benchmarking tool, we are able to easily visualize and optimize performance in our code precisely. Usually, we would have to use a while loop and Date.now or performance.now bindings to measure performance. Astral adds warmup periods, logging, and a few other tools. Lets get started! In an existing AssemblyScript project, install as-tral.

Next, create a benches folder in your assembly directory. Add a file named as-tral.d.ts in there.

assembly/
└── __benches__/
    └── as-tral.d.ts

In as-tral.d.ts, copy and paste

/// <reference path="../../node_modules/@as-tral/cli/as-tral.d.ts" />

Stick any file with a .ts extension in benches. You can even have multiple.

assembly/
└── __benches__/
    ├── as-tral.d.ts
    └── my-benchmark.ts

Your benchmark will live in this file. Let's create an example benchmark.

// to ensure accurate benchmarks, we must make sure that binaryen doesn't do any sneaky
// optimizations on our input without us knowing. Thus, we must use `blackbox`.
const input = blackbox("The quick brown fox jumped over the lazy dog.".repeat(10)); // our string here must be a compile time constant.
// open an issue if you'd like to see this constraint lifted.
bench("string split", () => { // this function body will be run many times. // we must make sure our compiler won't throw away the computation, // so we use `blackbox` here again. blackbox(input.split(" "));
});

Now, let's run as-tral.

rom@i9-cabin:~/demo$ npx astral
Compiling assembly/__benches__/hello.ts

Benchmarking string split: Warming up for 3000ms
Benchmarking string split: Collecting 100 samples in estimated 5018.6ms (1.2M iterations)
Benchmarking string split: Analyzing
string split            time: [3770.9ns 3775ns 3778.9ns]
Found 4 outliers among 100 measurements (4%)
  3 (3%) low mild
  1 (1%) high mild

Pretty great!

Mohammed Nadeem Shareef - Apr 22

Blogger and Programmer - May 9
item.author: jairus

item.title: 本地软件开发商案例 | The case of the local software developer
item.description:
所以它是关于本地软件开发人员的。

你可以将“本地软件”视为核心问题，也可以看到背后的“本地开发者”。 正如杰克斯派洛所说， 问题不是问题，问题是你对问题的态度。

幸运的是，这两种态度都足够好，是相关的，并且是对我向你们所有人提出的问题的补充。

让我们看一下这个例子。 假设您是一家银行的经销商，或者您是一名原始的 IT 专家，为经销商工作。 您需要进行一些在公司 IT 系统中根本不可用的计算，或者可用的太晚，或者不断出现一些错误和差距。

如果你很聪明——经销商通常也很聪明，我认识很多聪明的经销商，有些比我聪明——你可以自己做。

当然，通常有可能向本地 IT 部门发送请求并订购应用程序，但是……这不可能的原因有很多。 原因不重要。

让我们继续下一集，为了简短起见：我们决定在 Excel 和 VBA 中进行。 看起来很熟悉？ 毫无疑问。 我们甚至可以制定一个规则：

在每个重要的业务 IT 生态系统中，至少有一个 Excel 电子表格是最关键的核心流程的重要且不易移除的部分。

我不知道是否有人已经制定了这条规则，但我很确定我不能成为第一个。

进入下一个赛季：我们决定下一个此类应用程序，下一个，下一个......

这些应用程序有一些共同点，尤其是这些代码部分带有神圣的注释，什么也不说，或者说一些难以理解的东西，包括对更美好未来的声明（TODO），以及关于（XXX和HACK）中的危险咒语的警告。

我们都在那里，不是吗？ 也许我们忘记了，但我们的评论并没有忘记我们。

或多或少相似，通常这些应用程序是我们教育的里程碑，至少在 VBA 中，有时甚至在编程中。 因此，这些部件中的每一个都以另一种方式工作，并且需要不同的维护方法。

有时我们在这个系列中有一个更大的转折：我们构建了一个综合应用程序，它统治着所有的应用程序。

是的，我们非常非常了解这个故事，至少我们中的一些人是这样。

所以我的问题是：我们可以用另一种方式处理这个案子吗？ 更聪明的方法？ 程序员的方式？

也许不是，因为这个本地软件开发者案例通常是常见的软件开发者案例。 所以也许这是一个更大的问题，一个范式问题。

但也许有可能？

我有一个包含许多细节的一般概念，但现在我对收集意见非常感兴趣。 我从一些世界知名社区的类似问题开始：

简而言之，我发现程序员写了一些关于他们自己的职业生涯故事的有趣的评论，但反馈很差。 也许是我的错，我至少犯了一个错误，在 Stack Overflow 上征求意见 - 不要在家里这样做，虽然目标在一开始就明确定义，但我获得了禁令。

到岸边！ 您认为，是否有可能以更好的方式构建本地软件？ 或者，至少，您当地的软件开发人员的故事是什么？

PS It was my first post at dev.to, thank you very much for reading. PS 这是我在 dev.to 的第一篇文章，非常感谢您的阅读。



原文:

So it is about local software developers.

You can consider "the local software" as the core problem, or you can see "the local developer" behind it. As Jack Sparrow said, the problem is not the problem, the problem is your attitude about the problem.

Fortunately, these two attitudes are good enough, are relevant, and are complementary to my question to all of you.

Let's look at the example. Let's assume you are a dealer in a bank, or you are a raw IT specialist, working for the dealer. And you need to make some calculations not available at all in company IT systems, or available too late, or constantly incoming with some bugs and gaps.

If you are smart - and dealers are usually pretty smart, I know many smart dealers, some are smarter than me - you can do it by yourself.

Of course, there is usually a possibility to send a request to the local IT and order an application, but... there are many reasons why it could not be possible. The reason is unimportant.

Let's move forward to the next episode, to keep it short: we make a decision that we do it in Excel and VBA. Looks familiar? No doubt. We can even formulate a rule:

In every non-trivial business IT ecosystem, at least one Excel spreadsheet is an important, non-easily removable part of the most critical core process.

I do not know, whether someone already formulated this rule, but I am pretty sure that I cannot be the first.

Go to the next season: we make a decision about a next application of this kind, and a next, and a next...

The applications have some parts in common, especially these parts of code with holy curtains of comments, saying nothing, or saying something incomprehensible, including declarations of a better future (TODO), with warnings about dangerous spells within (XXX and HACK).

We were there, all of us, weren't we? Maybe we forget, but our comments do not forget about us.

Less or more similar, generally these applications are milestones of our education, at least in VBA, sometimes in programming at all. So every one of these pieces is working in another manner and requires a different approach to maintenance.

Sometimes we have a bigger twist in this serial: we build the one omnibus application, which rules all of the applications.

Yeah, we know this story very, very well, at least some of us.

So my question is: can we proceed with that case in another way? A smarter way? The programmer's way?

Maybe not, because this local software developer case is often the usual software developer case. So maybe this is a bigger problem, a paradigm problem.

But maybe it is possible?

I have a general concept with many details, but now I am pretty interested in gathering opinions. I have started with a similar question at some world-known communities:

In brief, I have found some nice and interesting comments by programmers writing about their own professional career stories, but the feedback was rather poor. Maybe it is my fault, I have made at least one mistake, asking for opinions at Stack Overflow - do not do this at home, I earned a ban although the goal was precisely defined at the beginning.

To the shore! What do you think, is it possible to build local software in a better way? Or, at least, what is your local software developer's story?

PS It was my first post at dev.to, thank you very much for reading.
item.author: gjaryczewski
